title: Oscars Surprise: Live-Action Short Results in Seventh Tie in Academy Awards’ History
content:
ByRebecca Rubin

Senior Film and Media Reporter

It’s a tie!

For only the seventh time inOscarshistory, a category ended with two winners after “The Singers” and “Two People Exchanging Saliva” both nabbed the Academy Award for live-action short film.

“Ironic that the short film Oscar is going to take twice as long,” Kumail Nanjiani, who presented the award, cracked as he read the winners. He called up the directors — Sam A. Davis and Jack Piatt for “The Singers” and Alexandre Singh and Natalie Musteata for “Two People Exchanging Saliva” — one at a time to give speeches.

“A tie — wow. I didn’t know that was a thing. but we’re happy to be up here,” said “The Singers” filmmakers.

In his speech, Singh took a jab at Timothee Chalamet’s recent viral commentsregarding his thoughtson the state of ballet and opera.

“We believe art can change people’s souls. Maybe it takes 10 years time, but we can change society through art, through creativity, through theater and ballet — and cinema.”

Prior to this year’s telecast, the most recent tie was at the 2013 ceremony when “Zero Dark Thirty” and “Skyfall” shared the award for sound editing. Other notable ties include 1932 when best actor went to Wallace Beery (“The Champ”) and Fredric March (“Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde”); 1949 when documentary short went to “A Chance to Live” and “So Much for So Little”; 1986 when documentary feature went to “Artie Shaw: Time Is All You’ve Got” and “Down and Out in America” and 1994 when live-action short film went to “Franz Kafka’s It’s a Wonderful Life” and “Trevor.” Perhaps most memorably, Katharine Hepburn (“The Lion in Winter”) and Barbra Streisand (“Funny Girl”) shared the best actress trophy at the 1968 awards.

According to the Academy, the first tie has a major asterisk. March had one more vote than Beery (only three men were even nominated!), but the rules at the time stated that “if an achievement came within three votes of the winner, that achievement would also receive the award.” Since then, it’s been changed so only an exact match in totals would qualify as a tie.
title_zh: 奧斯卡驚喜：真人短片結果成為奧斯卡歷史上第七次平局
content_zh:
由瑞貝卡·魯賓(Rebecca Rubin)報導

資深電影與媒體記者

平手了！

在奧斯卡歷史上，這是第七次出現某個類別有兩位獲獎者的情況，因為《歌者們》(The Singers)和《兩人交換唾液》(Two People Exchanging Saliva)都獲得了最佳真人短片的奧斯卡獎。

頒獎者庫梅爾·南賈尼(Kumail Nanjiani)在宣佈獲獎者時開玩笑說：“諷刺的是，這個短片奧斯卡將需要兩倍的時間。”他一一叫上導演——《歌者們》的薩姆·A·戴維斯(Sam A. Davis)和傑克·皮亞特(Jack Piatt)，以及《兩人交換唾液》的亞歷山大·辛格(Alexandre Singh)和娜塔莉·穆斯特塔(Natalie Musteata)上台發表演講。

《歌者們》的製作團隊表示：“平手——哇。我不知道這是可能的，但我們很高興能站在這裡。”

在演講中，辛格對提摩西·查拉梅(Timothee Chalamet)最近關於芭蕾舞和歌劇狀況的病毒式評論進行了調侃。

“我們相信藝術可以改變人們的靈魂。也許需要十年的時間，但我們可以通過藝術、創造力、劇院和芭蕾舞——以及電影來改變社會。”

在今年的直播之前，最近一次平手發生在2013年頒獎典禮上，當時《零黑三十》(Zero Dark Thirty)和《天幕危機》(Skyfall)分享了最佳音效剪輯獎。其他著名的平手案例包括1932年最佳男演員獎由華萊士·比瑞(Wallace Beery)《冠軍》(The Champ)和弗雷德里克·馬奇(Fredric March)《化身博士》(Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde)共同獲得；1949年紀錄短片獎由《生存的機會》(A Chance to Live)和《如此之少》(So Much for So Little)共同獲得；1986年紀錄長片獎由《阿提·肖：你擁有的只有時間》(Artie Shaw: Time Is All You’ve Got)和《美國的失落與絕望》(Down and Out in America)共同獲得；1994年最佳真人短片獎由《弗朗茲·卡夫卡的美好人生》(Franz Kafka’s It’s a Wonderful Life)和《特雷弗》(Trevor)共同獲得。也許最令人難忘的是，凱瑟琳·赫本(Katharine Hepburn)《寒冬獅王》(The Lion in Winter)和巴巴拉·史翠珊(Barbra Streisand)《搞笑女郎》(Funny Girl)在1968年的頒獎典禮上共同獲得最佳女主角獎。

根據電影學院的資料，第一次平手有一個重要的註解。馬奇的票數比比瑞多一票（當時只有三位男性被提名！），但當時的規則規定：“如果某項成就與獲獎者相差三票內，該成就也將獲得獎項。”自那時起，規則已更改，只有總票數完全相等的情況才會被視為平手。
summary_zh:
由瑞貝卡·魯賓(Rebecca Rubin)報導

資深電影與媒體記者

平手了！

在奧斯卡歷史上，這是第七次出現某個類別有兩位獲獎者的情況，因為《歌者們》(The Singers)和《兩人交換唾液》(Two People Exchanging Saliva)都獲得了最佳真人短片的奧斯卡獎。
